title: CLAUDE.md
text: autoedit R package providing real-time collaborative code editor widget built CodeMirror 6 Automerge CRDT. connects via WebSocket automerge-repo compatible sync servers multi-user editing automatic conflict resolution.

title: CLAUDE.md

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: run examples, need autoedit package installed: Open multiple browser windows tabs pointing Shiny app URL see real-time collaboration action.
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("shikokuchuo/autoedit")

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: editor() widget provides best collaborative editing experience. uses CodeMirror 6 automerge-codemirror integration, applies Automerge operations directly CodeMirror’s document model. means cursor stays place others edit, get full syntax highlighting, documents can saved restored across sessions.

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: following example uses autosync package, provides -process Automerge sync server:
code:
library(shiny) library(bslib) library(autoedit) library(autosync) library(automerge)  # Room-specific initial content templates room_templates <- list(   standup = "# Daily Standup

## What I did yesterday
- 

## What I'm doing today
- 

## Blockers
- 
",   planning = "# Sprint Planning

## Goals
- 

## User Stories
- 

## Capacity
- 
",   retrospective = "# Retrospective

## What went well
- 

## What could be improved
- 

## Action items
- 
",   brainstorm = "# Brainstorming Session

## Ideas
- 

## Discussion Notes
- 
" )  # Create and start the sync server (once, at app startup) sync_server <- amsync_server(port = 3030) sync_server$start()  # Create a document for each room with initial content doc_ids <- list() for (room in names(room_templates)) {   doc_ids[[room]] <- create_document(sync_server)   doc <- get_document(sync_server, doc_ids[[room]])   am_put(doc, AM_ROOT, "text", am_text(room_templates[[room]]))   am_commit(doc, "init") }  ui <- page_fillable(   padding = "1rem",   div(class = "d-flex justify-content-between align-items-center mb-3",     h2("Collaborative Meeting Notes", class = "mb-0"),     downloadButton("export", "Export as Quarto", class = "btn-sm")   ),   navset_card_pill(     id = "room",     nav_panel("Daily Standup", value = "standup", uiOutput("editor_standup")),     nav_panel("Sprint Planning", value = "planning", uiOutput("editor_planning")),     nav_panel("Retrospective", value = "retrospective", uiOutput("editor_retrospective")),     nav_panel("Brainstorming", value = "brainstorm", uiOutput("editor_brainstorm"))   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$editor_standup <- renderUI(editor_output("ed_standup", height = "500px"))   output$editor_planning <- renderUI(editor_output("ed_planning", height = "500px"))   output$editor_retrospective <- renderUI(editor_output("ed_retrospective", height = "500px"))   output$editor_brainstorm <- renderUI(editor_output("ed_brainstorm", height = "500px"))    output$ed_standup <- editor_render(editor(sync_server$url, doc_ids[["standup"]], height = "500px"))   output$ed_planning <- editor_render(editor(sync_server$url, doc_ids[["planning"]], height = "500px"))   output$ed_retrospective <- editor_render(editor(sync_server$url, doc_ids[["retrospective"]], height = "500px"))   output$ed_brainstorm <- editor_render(editor(sync_server$url, doc_ids[["brainstorm"]], height = "500px"))    current_content <- reactive({     switch(input$room,       "standup" = input$ed_standup_content,       "planning" = input$ed_planning_content,       "retrospective" = input$ed_retrospective_content,       "brainstorm" = input$ed_brainstorm_content     )   })    output$export <- downloadHandler(     filename = function() paste0(input$room, "-notes-", Sys.Date(), ".qmd"),     content = function(file) {       titles <- c(standup = "Daily Standup", planning = "Sprint Planning",                   retrospective = "Retrospective", brainstorm = "Brainstorming")       front_matter <- sprintf("---
title: "%s"
date: "%s"
format: html
---

",                               titles[input$room], Sys.Date())       writeLines(paste0(front_matter, current_content()), file)     }   ) }  # Clean up when app stops onStop(function() sync_server$close())  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: simpler use cases, autoedit provides textarea_ui() textarea_server() functions synchronize text without requiring external sync server.

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: textarea module uses Automerge CRDT (Conflict-free Replicated Data Type) synchronize text across multiple browser sessions. external server required - synchronization happens -process via Shiny’s reactive system. Concurrent edits multiple users merged automatically, can use different doc_id values create separate collaborative spaces.

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
code:
library(shiny) library(bslib) library(autoedit)  # Room-specific initial content templates room_templates <- list(   standup = "# Daily Standup

## What I did yesterday
- 

## What I'm doing today
- 

## Blockers
- 
",   planning = "# Sprint Planning

## Goals
- 

## User Stories
- 

## Capacity
- 
",   retrospective = "# Retrospective

## What went well
- 

## What could be improved
- 

## Action items
- 
",   brainstorm = "# Brainstorming Session

## Ideas
- 

## Discussion Notes
- 
" )  ui <- page_fillable(   padding = "1rem",   div(class = "d-flex justify-content-between align-items-center mb-3",     h2("Collaborative Meeting Notes", class = "mb-0"),     downloadButton("export", "Export as Quarto", class = "btn-sm")   ),   card(     card_header(       navset_pill(         id = "room",         nav_panel("Daily Standup", value = "standup"),         nav_panel("Sprint Planning", value = "planning"),         nav_panel("Retrospective", value = "retrospective"),         nav_panel("Brainstorming", value = "brainstorm")       )     ),     card_body(       conditionalPanel("input.room === 'standup'",         textarea_ui("notes_standup", label = NULL, width = "100%", height = "500px",                     placeholder = "Start typing your standup notes...")),       conditionalPanel("input.room === 'planning'",         textarea_ui("notes_planning", label = NULL, width = "100%", height = "500px",                     placeholder = "Start typing your planning notes...")),       conditionalPanel("input.room === 'retrospective'",         textarea_ui("notes_retrospective", label = NULL, width = "100%", height = "500px",                     placeholder = "Start typing your retrospective notes...")),       conditionalPanel("input.room === 'brainstorm'",         textarea_ui("notes_brainstorm", label = NULL, width = "100%", height = "500px",                     placeholder = "Start typing your ideas..."))     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   text_standup <- textarea_server("notes_standup", doc_id = "standup",                                   initial_text = room_templates$standup)   text_planning <- textarea_server("notes_planning", doc_id = "planning",                                    initial_text = room_templates$planning)   text_retrospective <- textarea_server("notes_retrospective", doc_id = "retrospective",                                         initial_text = room_templates$retrospective)   text_brainstorm <- textarea_server("notes_brainstorm", doc_id = "brainstorm",                                      initial_text = room_templates$brainstorm)    current_text <- reactive({     switch(input$room,       "standup" = text_standup(),       "planning" = text_planning(),       "retrospective" = text_retrospective(),       "brainstorm" = text_brainstorm()     )   })    output$export <- downloadHandler(     filename = function() paste0(input$room, "-notes-", Sys.Date(), ".qmd"),     content = function(file) {       titles <- c(standup = "Daily Standup", planning = "Sprint Planning",                   retrospective = "Retrospective", brainstorm = "Brainstorming")       front_matter <- sprintf("---
title: "%s"
date: "%s"
format: html
---

",                               titles[input$room], Sys.Date())       writeLines(paste0(front_matter, current_text()), file)     }   ) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: textarea_server() call takes doc_id parameter identifies collaborative document. sessions using doc_id synchronize together:
code: # These will sync together (same doc_id) textarea_server("editor1", doc_id = "shared-doc") textarea_server("editor2", doc_id = "shared-doc")  # This is independent (different doc_id) textarea_server("editor3", doc_id = "private-doc")

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: initial_text parameter sets starting content new document. document already exists (another session created ), parameter ignored:
code:
textarea_server(  "notes",  doc_id = "meeting",  initial_text = "# Meeting Notes

Attendees:
- " )

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: avoid excessive synchronization, text changes debounced. default 150ms, can adjust :
code: # Faster sync (more responsive, more overhead) textarea_server("fast", doc_id = "doc", debounce_ms = 50)  # Slower sync (less responsive, less overhead) textarea_server("slow", doc_id = "doc", debounce_ms = 500)

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: serverless textarea module limitations compared sync server-based editor:

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: textarea module uses Automerge efficiently CRDT layer - deltas (individual character insertions deletions) synchronized documents. However, HTML <textarea> elements API granular text updates. way update textarea programmatically replace entire .value property. means another user’s changes arrive: Automerge efficiently merges just changed operations merged text extracted full string textarea value replaced entirely cursor position lost truly smooth concurrent editing cursor preservation, need editor proper document model can apply operations incrementally - exactly editor() CodeMirror provides.

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: Synchronization works within single R process. run multiple Shiny processes (e.g., behind load balancer), sessions different processes won’t sync. multi-process deployments, use editor() sync server.

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: Documents stored memory lost R process restarts. persistent collaboration, use editor() sync server supports document storage.

what: Use editor() with a sync server when:
title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: Multiple users type simultaneously need syntax highlighting need document persistence ’re deploying across multiple processes Smooth cursor behavior important

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: want zero external dependencies Collaboration light (turn-taking rather simultaneous typing) occasional cursor jump acceptable ’re building quick prototype

title: Collaborative Text Editing for Shiny with Automerge
text: autoedit provides two approaches collaborative editing:

title: Collaborative Text Editing for Shiny with Automerge
text: Shiny module syncs across browser sessions using Shiny’s reactive system. external server required - Shiny process manages document state directly. Open multiple browser windows real-time collaboration.
code: library(shiny) library(autoedit)  ui <- fluidPage(   textarea_ui("editor", label = "Collaborative notes") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   text <- textarea_server("editor", initial_text = "Start typing...") }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Collaborative Text Editing for Shiny with Automerge
text: CodeMirror 6 code editor widget syncs via WebSocket automerge-repo compatible server. Suitable collaboration across different applications persistent documents.
code: library(autoedit)  # Connect to a sync server editor("ws://localhost:3030", "document-id")  # Or use the public Automerge sync server editor("wss://sync.automerge.org", "your-document-id")

title: Collaborative Text Editing for Shiny with Automerge
code: library(shiny) library(automerge) library(autosync) library(autoedit)  server <- amsync_server(port = 3030, host = "127.0.0.1") server$start()  doc_id <- create_document(server) doc <- get_document(server, doc_id) am_put(doc, AM_ROOT, "text", am_text("")) am_commit(doc, "init")  ui <- fluidPage(editor_output("editor"))  shiny_server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$editor <- editor_render(editor(server$url, doc_id)) }  onStop(function() server$close()) shinyApp(ui, shiny_server)

title: autoedit: Collaborative Text Editing for Shiny with Automerge — autoedit-package
text: Real-time collaborative text editing R Shiny applications, built 'Automerge' 'CRDT'. Provides 'CodeMirror' 6 editor widget WebSocket sync serverless Shiny module multi-user editing automatic conflict resolution.

title: Shiny bindings for editor — editor-shiny
text: Output render functions using editor within Shiny applications interactive R Markdown documents.

title: Shiny bindings for editor — editor-shiny
code: editor_output(outputId, width = "100%", height = "400px")  editor_render(expr, env = parent.frame(), quoted = FALSE)

title: Shiny bindings for editor — editor-shiny
text: outputId Output variable read . width, height Widget dimensions (must valid CSS unit number coerced string "px" appended). expr expression generates editor widget. env environment evaluate expr. quoted Logical, whether expr quoted expression.

title: Shiny bindings for editor — editor-shiny
text: editor_output() returns Shiny output element. editor_render() returns Shiny render function.

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
text: Creates real-time collaborative text editor using CodeMirror 6 Automerge CRDT, synchronized via WebSocket automerge-repo compatible sync server.

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
code: editor(server_url, doc_id, timeout = 10000, width = "100%", height = "400px")

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
text: server_url WebSocket URL (ws:// wss://) sync server. doc_id Document ID (base58check encoded). timeout Connection timeout milliseconds. Default 10000. width, height Widget dimensions.

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
text: htmlwidget object.

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
text: widget connects specified sync server synchronizes CodeMirror 6 editor Automerge document. Multiple users can edit document simultaneously automatic conflict resolution. Automerge document must "text" field type Automerge text (created automerge::am_text()). Shiny applications, current editor content available input value input$<outputId>_content.

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Connect to the public Automerge sync server editor("wss://sync.automerge.org", "your-document-id")  # Or use with an autosync server server <- autosync::amsync_server(port = 3030) server$start()  doc_id <- autosync::create_document(server) doc <- autosync::get_document(server, doc_id) automerge::am_put(doc, automerge::AM_ROOT, "text", automerge::am_text("")) automerge::am_commit(doc, "init")  editor(server$url, doc_id) } # }

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
text: Server logic collaborative textarea synchronizes across Shiny sessions using Automerge CRDT.

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
code: textarea_server(id, doc_id = "default", initial_text = "", debounce_ms = 150)

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
text: id Module ID (must match ID used textarea_ui()). doc_id Document identifier. textareas doc_id synchronize together. Default "default". initial_text Initial text content. used creating new document; ignored document already exists. debounce_ms Debounce delay milliseconds text input changes. Default 150ms.

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
text: reactive expression returning current synchronized text.

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
text: module uses Shiny's reactive system synchronize text across multiple browser sessions without requiring external sync server. session maintains local Automerge document syncs shared master document using Automerge's sync protocol. Concurrent edits automatically merged using Automerge's CRDT algorithm, ensuring eventual consistency across sessions.

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
code: if (interactive()) {   ui <- shiny::fluidPage(textarea_ui("editor"))   server <- function(input, output, session) textarea_server("editor")   shiny::shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Collaborative textarea UI — textarea_ui
text: Creates textarea input synchronizes across Shiny sessions using Automerge CRDT, without requiring external sync server.

title: Collaborative textarea UI — textarea_ui
code: textarea_ui(   id,   label = NULL,   width = "100%",   height = "200px",   placeholder = NULL )

title: Collaborative textarea UI — textarea_ui
text: id Module ID. label Label textarea, NULL label. width width input (e.g., "100%", "400px"). height height input (e.g., "200px"). placeholder Placeholder text empty.

title: Collaborative textarea UI — textarea_ui
text: Shiny UI element.

title: Collaborative textarea UI — textarea_ui
code: if (interactive()) {   ui <- shiny::fluidPage(textarea_ui("editor"))   server <- function(input, output, session) textarea_server("editor")   shiny::shinyApp(ui, server) }
